task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window is open with a protein structure visible, and '1D3Z' or '1d3z' is listed in the right-hand object menu.

task_completion.description: Align the 10 NMR states to a reference state
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ensemble of 10 states is visible in the PyMOL viewer, overlapping closely in the core but diverging at the termini/loops. PyMOL command line or history may show alignment commands (e.g., 'intra_fit' or 'align').

task_completion.description: Color the structure by per-residue flexibility
task_completion.visual_evidence: The protein in PyMOL is colored with a gradient (e.g., spectrum coloring or putty representation) where flexible regions (like the C-terminus) are distinctly colored or thicker than the rigid core.

task_completion.description: Save a PNG figure of the colored ensemble to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or PyMOL command line shows the file 'ubiquitin_ensemble.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/PyMOL_Data/images/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a plain-text report identifying flexible and rigid regions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows a report file being edited or saved to '~/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_flexibility_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: List the top 5 most flexible residues with numeric RMSD values in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a list of exactly 5 residues (likely in the 72-76 range) with associated numeric values representing RMSD or flexibility.

task_completion.description: Explicitly mention the total number of models/states in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text report contains a statement explicitly mentioning '10 models', '10 states', or '10 conformers'.

integrity.description: Agent used PyMOL to perform the alignment and coloring
integrity.visual_evidence: PyMOL GUI is visible with the 1D3Z structure loaded, showing evidence of actual manipulation (e.g., command history, object panel changes) rather than just viewing a static image.

integrity.description: Agent created the report based on actual data
integrity.visual_evidence: The text editor shows the report being actively edited or generated, with specific RMSD values that appear to be derived from the PyMOL analysis rather than generic placeholder text.

integrity.description: Agent saved files to the exact requested paths
integrity.visual_evidence: The file paths visible in the terminal, file manager, or text editor exactly match '~/PyMOL_Data/images/ubiquitin_ensemble.png' and '~/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_flexibility_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on PDB ID: 1D3Z, which contains exactly 10 conformer models. The extreme C-terminal (residues 72-76) is expected to be the most flexible region. The figure must be saved to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/ubiquitin_ensemble.png and the report to /home/ga/PyMOL_Data/ubiquitin_flexibility_report.txt.
